# Effortless AWS

> Code-first AWS Lambda framework for TypeScript. Export handlers, deploy with one command.

Effortless AWS is a TypeScript framework where you define AWS resources (Lambda, DynamoDB, SQS, S3, SES, CloudFront) by exporting handler objects from `.ts` files. The framework handles all infrastructure — IAM roles, Lambda Function URLs, event source mappings, Lambda layers — automatically. No YAML, no CloudFormation, no state files.

- Package: `effortless-aws` (npm)
- CLI: `eff deploy`
- Docs: https://effortless-aws.website

## Project setup

```typescript
// effortless.config.ts
import { defineConfig } from "effortless-aws";

export default defineConfig({
  name: "my-service",
  region: "eu-central-1",
  handlers: ["src/**/*.ts"],
  stage: "dev",
  lambda: {
    memory: 256,
    timeout: "30 seconds",
    runtime: "nodejs24.x",
  },
});
```

All Lambdas run on ARM64 (Graviton2). Not configurable.

## Core concepts

### Handler = exported const

Every `export const x = define*({...})` creates AWS resources. The export name becomes the resource name.

### deps = automatic IAM wiring

Reference other handlers via `deps: () => ({ orders, uploads, mailer })`. The framework injects typed clients and creates IAM permissions automatically.

### config = SSM secrets

`config: { key: param("ssm/path") }` fetches secrets from SSM Parameter Store at cold start, caches them. SSM path: `/${project}/${stage}/${key}`.

### setup = cold-start factory

`setup: async ({ deps, config }) => ({ db: createPool(config.dbUrl) })` runs once per cold start. Return value properties are **spread directly into callback args** (no `ctx` wrapper). `deps`, `config`, and `files` are available in `setup` only — not in callbacks.

### schema = validation

Pass a validation function for runtime validation, or use the generic parameter on `define*<T>()` for type-only. Validated data arrives as `data` arg in callbacks.

### static = bundled files

`static: ["src/templates/*.ejs"]` bundles files into Lambda ZIP. Access via `files.read("src/templates/invoice.ejs")`.

### Shared handler options

All handler types accept: `deps`, `config`, `setup`, `memory`, `timeout`, `logLevel`, `onError`, `onCleanup`. Examples below show these only where relevant — they work the same everywhere.

## Handler types

### defineApi — HTTP API

Creates Lambda + Function URL. Routes via chained `.get()`, `.post()`, `.put()`, `.delete()`, `.patch()` with path as first arg. Shared options in the constructor; `.setup()` is chained.

```typescript
import { defineApi, defineTable, param } from "effortless-aws";
import { z } from "zod";

type Session = { userId: string; role: string };
export const users = defineTable<{ tag: string; name: string }>().build();

export default defineApi({
  deps: () => ({ users }),
  config: { dbUrl: param("database-url") },
})
  .setup(async ({ deps, config }) => {
    const pool = createPool(config.dbUrl);
    const auth = enableAuth<Session>({ expiresIn: "7d" });
    return { pool, auth };
  })
  .get("/api/users", async ({ pool }) => ({
    status: 200,
    body: await pool.query("SELECT * FROM users"),
  }))
  .post("/api/users", async ({ input, pool }) => {
    const data = z.object({ name: z.string() }).parse(input);
    return { status: 201, body: { id: crypto.randomUUID() } };
  })
  .post("/api/login", async ({ input, auth }) => {
    // public: true skips auth check
    return auth.createSession({ userId: "admin", role: "admin" });
  }, { public: true });
```

**Route options:** `.get("/path", handler)` — authenticated by default. `.post("/path", handler, { public: true })` — public. Unmatched routes → 404, missing session → 401.

**`enableAuth`** — return from `.setup()` to enable typed auth. Helpers spread into route args:
- `auth.createSession(data)` — set signed cookie, returns `{ status, headers }`
- `auth.clearSession()` — clear cookie
- `auth.session` — current session (`T | undefined`)
- Supports hybrid API token auth: `enableAuth<S>({ expiresIn: "7d", apiToken: { verify: fn, cacheTtl: "5m", header: "x-api-key" } })`

**`req` object:** `req.method`, `req.path`, `req.headers`, `req.query`, `req.body` (auto-parsed JSON), `req.rawBody`.

### defineTable — DynamoDB table

Creates a DynamoDB table with fixed envelope: `{ pk, sk, tag, data: T, ttl? }`. Optionally creates a stream Lambda via `onRecord`/`onRecordBatch`.

```typescript
import { defineTable } from "effortless-aws";

type Order = { tag: string; amount: number; status: string };

// Resource-only
export const orders = defineTable<Order>().build();

// With stream handler (builder chain)
export const orders = defineTable<Order>({ batchSize: 100 })
  .deps(() => ({ customers }))
  .setup(({ deps }) => ({ customers: deps.customers }))
  .onRecord(async ({ record, customers }) => {
    // record.eventName: "INSERT" | "MODIFY" | "REMOVE"
    // record.new?.data / record.old?.data — typed as Order
    // record.keys — { pk, sk }
  });
// OR: .onRecordBatch (return { failures: string[] } for partial batch failure)
```

**TableClient API** (via `deps`):

```typescript
await table.put({ pk: "USER#1", sk: "PROFILE", data: { tag: "user", name: "Alice" } });
await table.put({ pk: "...", sk: "...", data: {...}, ttl: ts }, { ifNotExists: true });
const item = await table.get({ pk: "USER#1", sk: "PROFILE" }); // item.data — typed
await table.delete({ pk: "USER#1", sk: "PROFILE" });
await table.query({ pk: "USER#1", sk: { begins_with: "ORDER#" }, limit: 10, scanIndexForward: false });
await table.queryByTag({ tag: "user", limit: 50 }); // GSI
await table.update({ pk: "USER#1", sk: "ORDER#1" }, {
  set: { status: "shipped" }, append: { events: ["shipped"] }, remove: ["tempNotes"], tag: "shipped-order", ttl: ts,
});
```

### defineFifoQueue — SQS FIFO queue

Creates SQS FIFO queue + Lambda consumer.

```typescript
import { defineFifoQueue } from "effortless-aws";

type OrderEvent = { orderId: string; action: "created" | "shipped" };

export const orderQueue = defineFifoQueue<OrderEvent>({
  batchSize: 10,                    // 1-10
  batchWindow: 10,                  // seconds, 0-300
  visibilityTimeout: 120,
  contentBasedDeduplication: true,
})
  .onMessage(async ({ message }) => {
    // message.body — typed as OrderEvent
    // message.messageId, message.messageGroupId
  });
// OR: .onMessageBatch (return { failures: string[] } for partial batch failure)
```

**Sending** (via `deps`): `await orderQueue.send({ body: { orderId: "1", action: "created" }, messageGroupId: "order-1" })`

### defineBucket — S3 bucket

Creates S3 bucket. Optionally creates event Lambda.

```typescript
import { defineBucket } from "effortless-aws";

export const uploads = defineBucket({}); // resource-only

export const images = defineBucket({
  prefix: "uploads/", suffix: ".jpg",
  onObjectCreated: async ({ event, bucket }) => {
    // event.key, event.size, event.eTag, event.eventTime
    const file = await bucket.get(event.key);
  },
  onObjectRemoved: async ({ event }) => { /* ... */ },
});
```

**BucketClient API** (via `deps`): `put(key, content, opts?)`, `get(key)`, `delete(key)`, `list(prefix)`.

### defineCron — Scheduled Lambda

Creates EventBridge Scheduler + Lambda. Builder chain: `.deps()` → `.config()` → `.include()` → `.setup()` → `.onError()` → `.onCleanup()` → `.onTick()` (terminal).

```typescript
import { defineCron } from "effortless-aws";

export const cleanup = defineCron({ schedule: "rate(2 hours)" })
  .onTick(async () => { /* ... */ });

export const sync = defineCron({ schedule: "cron(0 9 * * ? *)", timezone: "Europe/Moscow" })
  .deps(() => ({ orders }))
  .setup(async ({ deps }) => ({ db: deps.orders }), { memory: 512, timeout: "5m" })
  .onTick(async ({ db }) => { /* ... */ });
```

**Schedule:** `"rate(5 minutes)"`, `"rate(1 hour)"`, `"rate(1 day)"` | `"cron(0 9 * * ? *)"` (6 fields: min hour dom month dow year). Supports `timezone` option with full IANA timezone autocomplete (418 zones).

### defineMailer — SES email

Creates SES email identity with DKIM. Resource-only, used via `deps`.

```typescript
export const mailer = defineMailer({ domain: "myapp.com" });
// Via deps: await mailer.send({ from, to, subject, html?, text? })
```

### defineApp — SSR framework deployment

Creates CloudFront + Lambda Function URL + S3 for SSR frameworks.

```typescript
export const frontend = defineApp({
  server: ".output/server",   // directory with index.mjs handler
  assets: ".output/public",   // static assets → S3
  build: "nuxt build",
  domain: "app.example.com",  // optional (needs ACM cert in us-east-1)
  memory: 1024,
});
// Stage-specific: domain: { prod: "app.example.com", staging: "staging.example.com" }
```

Supports: Nuxt (native), Next.js (via OpenNext), any Nitro-based framework, custom `handler(event, context)`.

### defineStaticSite — static site / SPA on CloudFront

Creates S3 + CloudFront distribution.

```typescript
export const docs = defineStaticSite({
  dir: "dist",
  build: "npm run build",
  domain: "docs.example.com",
  spa: true,                  // all routes → index.html
  errorPage: "404.html",      // non-SPA only
  routes: { "/api/*": apiHandler }, // proxy to API (eliminates CORS)
  middleware: async (request) => {  // Lambda@Edge auth
    if (!request.cookies.session) return { redirect: "/login" };
  },
});
```

## CLI commands

```bash
eff deploy                         # deploy all handlers
eff deploy ./src/api.ts            # deploy specific file
eff deploy createUser              # deploy specific handler
eff deploy --stage prod            # deploy to production
eff status                         # compare code vs deployed
eff logs processOrder --tail       # stream CloudWatch logs
eff logs processOrder --since 1h   # logs from last hour
eff config                         # interactive SSM setup
eff config list                    # show all params
eff config set stripe/secret-key   # set specific parameter
eff cleanup                        # preview orphaned resources
eff cleanup --all                  # delete all resources
eff layer                          # show dependency layer info
eff layer --build                  # build layer locally
```

All commands accept `--project`, `--stage`, `--region`, `--verbose`.

## Key patterns and conventions

### Naming

All AWS resources follow `${project}-${stage}-${handlerName}`: Lambda `my-service-dev-createUser`, DynamoDB `my-service-dev-orders`, SQS `my-service-dev-orderQueue.fifo`.

### Stage isolation

Each stage gets fully independent resources. No shared infrastructure between `dev` and `prod`.

### No state files

Resources are discovered via AWS resource tags: `effortless-project`, `effortless-stage`, `effortless-handler`, `effortless-component`.

### Lambda layer

Production `dependencies` from `package.json` → shared Lambda Layer. AWS SDK v3 excluded (provided by runtime). Hash-versioned, re-uploaded only when deps change.

### Callback args

Handler callbacks receive a single object. Args are conditionally present based on configuration:
- `req` — HTTP request (defineApi only)
- `data` — validated input (when schema is set) | `input` — raw input (without schema)
- `...ctx` — setup return properties spread directly into args
- `auth` — session helpers (when `enableAuth` returned from setup)
- `record` / `records` / `message` / `messages` / `event` / `bucket` — event data (type-specific)

`deps`, `config`, `files` are available in `setup` only — not in callbacks.

### param() and schema

```typescript
config: {
  apiKey: param("api-key"),                    // string
  port: param("port", Number),                 // number
  config: param("app-config", JSON.parse),     // parsed JSON
}
defineTable<MyType>()                          // type-only (no runtime validation)
schema: (input) => MySchema.parse(input)       // runtime validation (e.g. Zod)
```
